title: Power Integrations Inc (POWI) Q1 2021 Earnings Call Transcript
text: Power Integrations reported exceptional Q1 2021 results with revenues reaching $174 million, representing 58% year-over-year growth and 15% sequential increase. The company's non-GAAP operating margin expanded to 28.6%, with earnings per share doubling to $0.76. CEO Balu Balakrishnan attributed the strong performance to robust demand across all four end-market categories: communications, computer, industrial, and consumer. Communications category revenues were up more than 170% year-over-year, driven primarily by advanced charger adoption and market share gains. The company demonstrated particular strength in gallium nitride (GaN) technology for power applications. Management projected GaN revenues would grow at least three times compared to the prior year, supported by designs for high-efficiency fast chargers and emerging applications in automotive and industrial markets. Regarding supply chain dynamics, internal inventory decreased to 92 days while channel inventory fell to approximately two weeks. The company maintained capacity through partnerships with Japanese foundries and proprietary manufacturing processes. For Q2 2021, management guided for flat revenues compared to Q1, plus or minus 5%, which would represent over 60% year-over-year growth. Gross margins were expected to improve to the 50% to 50.5% range. Jennifer Lloyd's appointment to the board was announced.
